scene_input.scene_summary: Abū Ṭālib teaches Muhammad how to ride a horse, showing patience and encouragement.
scene_input.key_visual_action: Abū Ṭālib guiding Muhammad on the horse

schema_output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, p. 32
schema_output.moral_lesson: The importance of patience and encouragement in teaching

characters.name: Abū Ṭālib
characters.appearance: medium build, dignified upright posture, wearing a flowing white thobe with a deep blue woolen cloak draped over both shoulders and a white turban with a cloth tail hanging between the shoulder blades

characters.name: Muhammad
characters.appearance: slender build, slightly stooped shoulders, wearing a light brown thobe and plain beige woolen cloak with a white cloth head covering

schema_output.narrative_text: Abū Ṭālib teaches Muhammad how to ride a horse, showing him patience and encouragement as he tries to balance in the saddle.
schema_output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor storybook illustration, soft warm hand-painted style for ages 6-12. A serene open field near a house with unpainted mud-brick walls in warm earth tones and a thatched roof. Early morning sunlight casts a warm glow across the scene. In the center, a tall man in a deep blue woolen cloak is seen from behind, guiding a young woman on a horse - the woman's face is also not visible, wearing a light brown thobe and plain beige woolen cloak with a white cloth head covering. The horse's reins are in the man's right hand, with his left hand placed gently on the woman's back for support. A few scattered trees and a distant mountain range can be seen in the background. Soft warm morning light, peaceful and serene mood. Medium shot from a slight angle, looking down at the scene. Earthy, mossy, and sky blue palette with the deep blue cloak as a color anchor. No modern objects, historically accurate pre-central figure scene.
